verses.text: When Israel left Egypt, the Red Sea fled, the Jordan turned back, and the mountains 'skipped like rams.' Creation moved at God's redeeming work. The exodus shook the very earth before the presence of God.

When Israel left Egypt, the Red Sea fled, the Jordan turned back, and the mountains 'skipped like rams.' Creation moved at God's redeeming work. The exodus shook the very earth before the presence of God.

When Israel left Egypt, the Red Sea fled, the Jordan turned back, and the mountains 'skipped like rams.' Creation moved at God's redeeming work. The exodus shook the very earth before the presence of God.

When Israel left Egypt, the Red Sea fled, the Jordan turned back, and the mountains 'skipped like rams.' Creation moved at God's redeeming work. The exodus shook the very earth before the presence of God.

The psalmist asks the sea, mountains, and earth why they trembled — at 'the presence of the Lord,' who turned the rock into a pool of water. All nature bows before Him. The God who freed His people commands both sea and stone.
